title: fix: use global pricing for Claude Opus 4.8
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Switches Claude Opus 4.8 Bedrock routing to `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8`
- Uses AWS/Anthropic global rates as the cost basis before the `1.5x` multiplier
- Updates the effective pricing snapshot and parameter transform test fixture

Verified:
- `npx biome check --write shared/registry/text.ts gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/configs/modelConfigs.ts gen.pollinations.ai/test/text/transforms/parameterProcessor.test.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/__snapshots__/effective-prices.snapshot.test.ts.snap`
- `npx vitest run test/effective-prices.snapshot.test.ts`
- `npx vitest run test/text/transforms/parameterProcessor.test.ts`

gist.headline: Global Opus routing
gist.blurb: We've updated the routing and pricing calculations for Claude Opus 4.8 to use the correct global rates. Just a little housekeeping to keep the pollen math accurate!
gist.summary: Updated Claude Opus 4.8 Bedrock routing to use `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8`, ensuring the 1.5x multiplier is applied to the correct AWS/Anthropic global rates.
gist.impact: Pollen credit deductions for Claude Opus 4.8 will now accurately reflect the global pricing tier.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny accountant visor, adjusting a price tag on a retro terminal showing a globe. Nomnom eating a receipt.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny accountant visor, adjusting a glowing price tag on a retro terminal that displays a pixelated globe. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a long paper receipt. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
